title: The Game Audio Tutorial
creator: Richard Stevens and Dave Raybould
description: A practical introduction to sound and music for interactive games, supported by exercises and example material. It moves from basic engine concepts through ambience, effects, dialogue, music, and procedural techniques.
useful: A structured first course for someone who understands audio but has not yet built an interactive sound system.
audience: Students, composers, and sound designers taking their first steps into game-audio implementation.
learning: Understand the structure of interactive game audio
Implement common effects, ambience, and music behaviors
Use exercises to move from theory to a working scene
